skill.name: ai-automation-workflows
skill.description: Build automated AI workflows combining multiple models and services. Patterns: batch processing, scheduled tasks, event-driven pipelines, agent loops. Tools: inference.sh CLI, bash scripting, Python SDK, webhook integration. Use for: content automation, data processing, monitoring, scheduled generation.
skill.summary: Learn to build AI-powered automation workflows using bash scripts, Python SDK, and the inference.sh platform for batch processing, scheduled tasks, and event-driven pipelines.
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: All 80 static findings are false positives from legitimate documentation. The skill teaches AI automation workflows using bash scripts and Python as educational examples. External commands (shell backticks, subprocess.run) are part of documentation code blocks showing safe automation patterns. Network references are links to inference.sh platform documentation. No evidence of malicious intent, credential exfiltration, or unauthorized system modification. This is a legitimate educational skill for building AI-powered automation pipelines.

low_findings.title: Shell Command Documentation
low_findings.description: The skill contains 66 external command patterns in bash documentation blocks. All are legitimate educational examples showing safe automation patterns. Commands use hardcoded arguments with no user input injection vectors.

content.user_title: Build Automated AI Workflows at Scale
content.value_statement: Stop manually running AI tasks. Learn to build automated pipelines that batch process content, schedule daily generation, and chain multiple AI models together for powerful workflows.
actual_capabilities: Teaches five automation patterns: batch processing, sequential pipelines, parallel processing, conditional workflows, and retry with fallback
Provides complete bash script examples for daily content generation, scheduled cron jobs, and monitoring workflows
Includes Python SDK automation templates using subprocess.run for production-ready pipelines
Shows error handling, logging, and alerting patterns for robust automation
Demonstrates integration with multiple AI models through inference.sh CLI tool
limitations: Requires installation of inference.sh CLI tool separately from the skill
All examples use external API calls to inference.sh platform which may incur costs
Cron job setup requires system-level permissions not provided by the skill itself
Python automation examples require Python 3.6+ and dependencies to be installed manually

use_cases.title: Content Marketing Automation
use_cases.description: Generate a week of social media content, blog posts, and images automatically. Schedule daily generation and publish across platforms without manual intervention.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and content marketers

use_cases.title: Data Processing Pipeline
use_cases.description: Process large datasets through AI analysis automatically. Batch process files, generate insights, and save results with error handling and retry logic.

use_cases.title: Scheduled Report Generation
use_cases.description: Automate daily, weekly, or monthly reports using AI. Combine data analysis, summarization, and visualization generation in scheduled workflows.
use_cases.target_user: Business intelligence professionals and operations teams

prompt_templates.title: Basic Batch Image Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a bash script that generates 10 images using different prompts. Use the batch processing pattern from this skill. Add rate limiting with 2 second delays between requests.
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate multiple AI images in bulk with proper rate limiting

prompt_templates.title: Content Creation Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a sequential pipeline that: 1) Researches a topic using AI search, 2) Writes a blog post with Claude, 3) Generates a header image, 4) Creates social media posts. Use the skill's sequential pipeline pattern.
prompt_templates.scenario: Chain multiple AI models for end-to-end content creation

prompt_templates.title: Scheduled Daily Automation
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a bash script that runs every morning at 9 AM. It should generate a motivational quote image and a productivity tip. Save outputs to dated directories. Include the crontab entry I need to add.
prompt_templates.scenario: Set up cron-based daily AI content generation

prompt_templates.title: Error-Resilient Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an automation script with retry logic and fallback models. If the primary AI model fails, retry 3 times with exponential backoff, then fall back to an alternative model. Include logging to track all attempts.
prompt_templates.scenario: Build robust automation that handles failures gracefully

output_examples.input: Create a daily content generator script
output: # Generates daily quote image and tip

output_examples.input: Show me how to process 50 images in parallel
output: # Wait for all jobs to complete

best_practices: Always add rate limiting with sleep commands between API calls to avoid hitting rate limits
Implement comprehensive logging with timestamps to track workflow execution and debug issues
Test automation scripts manually before adding them to cron or scheduled jobs
anti_patterns: Do not run untested scripts in cron jobs - always verify they work correctly when run manually first
Avoid hardcoding API keys or sensitive data in scripts - use environment variables or config files
Never skip error handling - unhandled failures in automation can silently break entire pipelines

faq.question: Do I need to install anything before using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to install the inference.sh CLI tool first. The skill provides the install command which downloads the binary and verifies its checksum. No other dependencies are required for bash examples.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without paying for AI API calls?
faq.answer: No, the skill teaches automation using the inference.sh platform which requires API usage. Each automation you run will consume credits based on the models you use. Check pricing on inference.sh before running large batch jobs.

faq.question: What is the difference between sequential and parallel patterns?
faq.answer: Sequential patterns run one AI operation after another, using the output of each step as input to the next. Parallel patterns launch multiple AI operations simultaneously using background processes, then wait for all to complete. Use parallel when operations are independent.

faq.question: How do I schedule workflows to run automatically?
faq.answer: Use cron jobs on Linux/Mac or Task Scheduler on Windows. The skill shows the crontab format for scheduling scripts at specific times. Add your script path to crontab with the schedule expression for when you want it to run.

faq.question: What happens if an AI model fails during automation?
faq.answer: The skill teaches retry patterns with exponential backoff and fallback models. Implement error handling that checks return codes, retries failed operations, and falls back to alternative models if the primary fails after maximum attempts.

faq.question: Can I chain multiple different AI models together?
faq.answer: Yes, the sequential pipeline pattern shows how to combine models. For example, use a search model for research, Claude for writing, Flux for images, and another Claude model for social posts. Each model's output becomes input for the next step.
